<pr_description>
Consider the following PR description:
Django 3.2.4+ autoreload breaks on empty string in TEMPLATES DIRS.
Description
	
Django versions > 3.2.3 changes the way template dirs are handled, they are now normalized using pathlib.Path.
People having an invalid value in TEMPLATESDIRS? will notice that autoreload stops working.
"DIRS": os.getenv("TEMPLATES_DIRS", "").split(",") # wrong, should be filter(None, os.getenv("TEMPLATES_DIRS", "").split(","))
or anything else that produces this:
"DIRS": [''] # wrong
will break autoreload.
This happens because django/template/autoreload.py::template_changed was previously comparing the empty string to a directory, and would never match. Now the normalization transforms the empty string into the root of the project. The result is that template_changed() will now always return True, preventing the autoreload when the app code changes
Change that produced the regression
https://code.djangoproject.com/ticket/32744
Commits in main and stable/3.2.x:
​https://github.com/django/django/commit/68357b2ca9e88c40fc00d848799813241be39129
​https://github.com/django/django/commit/c0d506f5ef253f006dbff0b0092c8eecbd45eedf
Previous reports
[Server Reload Error...](https://code.djangoproject.com/ticket/33285)
[Auto-reload not detecting changes in Django 3.2](https://code.djangoproject.com/ticket/33266)
[Autoreloader doesn't work on Windows 10](https://code.djangoproject.com/ticket/32630)

</pr_description>

from pathlib import Path

from django.dispatch import receiver
from django.template import engines
from django.template.backends.django import DjangoTemplates
from django.utils._os import to_path
from django.utils.autoreload import autoreload_started, file_changed, is_django_path


def get_template_directories():
    # Iterate through each template backend and find
    # any template_loader that has a 'get_dirs' method.
    # Collect the directories, filtering out Django templates.
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    items = set()
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue

        items.update(cwd / to_path(dir) for dir in backend.engine.dirs)

        for loader in backend.engine.template_loaders:
            if not hasattr(loader, "get_dirs"):
                continue
            items.update(
                cwd / to_path(directory)
                for directory in loader.get_dirs()
                if not is_django_path(directory)
            )
    return items


def reset_loaders():
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue
        for loader in backend.engine.template_loaders:
            loader.reset()


@receiver(autoreload_started, dispatch_uid="template_loaders_watch_changes")
def watch_for_template_changes(sender, **kwargs):
    for directory in get_template_directories():
        sender.watch_dir(directory, "**/*")


@receiver(file_changed, dispatch_uid="template_loaders_file_changed")
def template_changed(sender, file_path, **kwargs):
    if file_path.suffix == ".py":
        return
    for template_dir in get_template_directories():
        if template_dir in file_path.parents:
            reset_loaders()
            return True
</output>

Now I can see the issue. The `get_template_directories()` function uses `to_path(dir)` to normalize directories. If `dir` is an empty string `""`, `to_path("")` will convert it to `Path("")` which then becomes `cwd / Path("")` = `cwd` (the current working directory). This means the template directory becomes the project root, and `template_changed` will always return True because any file path will have the project root in its parents.

Let me look at the `to_path` function:

def to_path(value):
    """Convert value to a pathlib.Path instance, if not already a Path."""
    if isinstance(value, Path):
        return value
    elif not isinstance(value, str):
        raise TypeError("Invalid path type: %s" % type(value).__name__)
    return Path(value)
</output>

Now I understand the issue clearly. When `dir` is an empty string `""`, `Path("")` creates a path representing the current directory (`.`), and when combined with `cwd / Path("")`, it becomes `cwd` itself. This causes `template_changed` to always return True because any file path will have the project root in its parents.

The fix should filter out empty strings in `get_template_directories()`. Let me also check the commit that introduced the regression:

from pathlib import Path

from django.dispatch import receiver
from django.template import engines
from django.template.backends.django import DjangoTemplates
from django.utils._os import to_path
from django.utils.autoreload import autoreload_started, file_changed, is_django_path


def get_template_directories():
    # Iterate through each template backend and find
    # any template_loader that has a 'get_dirs' method.
    # Collect the directories, filtering out Django templates.
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    items = set()
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue

        items.update(cwd / to_path(dir) for dir in backend.engine.dirs)

        for loader in backend.engine.template_loaders:
            if not hasattr(loader, "get_dirs"):
                continue
            items.update(
                cwd / to_path(directory)
                for directory in loader.get_dirs()
                if not is_django_path(directory)
            )
    return items


def reset_loaders():
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue
        for loader in backend.engine.template_loaders:
            loader.reset()


@receiver(autoreload_started, dispatch_uid="template_loaders_watch_changes")
def watch_for_template_changes(sender, **kwargs):
    for directory in get_template_directories():
        sender.watch_dir(directory, "**/*")


@receiver(file_changed, dispatch_uid="template_loaders_file_changed")
def template_changed(sender, file_path, **kwargs):
    if file_path.suffix == ".py":
        return
    for template_dir in get_template_directories():
        if template_dir in file_path.parents:
            reset_loaders()
            return True
</output>

from pathlib import Path

from django.dispatch import receiver
from django.template import engines
from django.template.backends.django import DjangoTemplates
from django.utils._os import to_path
from django.utils.autoreload import autoreload_started, file_changed, is_django_path


def get_template_directories():
    # Iterate through each template backend and find
    # any template_loader that has a 'get_dirs' method.
    # Collect the directories, filtering out Django templates.
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    items = set()
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue

        items.update(cwd / to_path(dir) for dir in backend.engine.dirs if dir)

        for loader in backend.engine.template_loaders:
            if not hasattr(loader, "get_dirs"):
                continue
            items.update(
                cwd / to_path(directory)
                for directory in loader.get_dirs()
                if not is_django_path(directory)
            )
    return items


def reset_loaders():
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue
        for loader in backend.engine.template_loaders:
            loader.reset()


@receiver(autoreload_started, dispatch_uid="template_loaders_watch_changes")
def watch_for_template_changes(sender, **kwargs):
    for directory in get_template_directories():
        sender.watch_dir(directory, "**/*")


@receiver(file_changed, dispatch_uid="template_loaders_file_changed")
def template_changed(sender, file_path, **kwargs):
    if file_path.suffix == ".py":
        return
    for template_dir in get_template_directories():
        if template_dir in file_path.parents:
            reset_loaders()
            return True

{"command": "cd /testbed && python -c "
import os
os.environ.setdefault('DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE', 'tests.test_sqlite')

import django
django.setup()

from django.template import engines
from django.template.backends.django import DjangoTemplates
from django.test.utils import override_settings

# Check what the default template dirs are
print('Before override:')
for backend in engines.all():
    if isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
        print('  dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)

with override_settings(TEMPLATES=[{
    'DIRS': [''],
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    print('During override:')
    for backend in engines.all():
        if isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            print('  dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)
    
    from django.template import autoreload
    dirs = autoreload.get_template_directories()
    print('  template dirs:', dirs)
""}

def get_template_directories():
    # Iterate through each template backend and find
    # any template_loader that has a 'get_dirs' method.
    # Collect the directories, filtering out Django templates.
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    items = set()
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue

        items.update(cwd / to_path(dir) for dir in backend.engine.dirs if dir)

        for loader in backend.engine.template_loaders:
            if not hasattr(loader, "get_dirs"):
                continue
            items.update(
                cwd / to_path(directory)
                for directory in loader.get_dirs()
                if not is_django_path(directory)
            )
    return items

{"command": "cd /testbed && python -c "
import os
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
django.setup()

from django.template import engines
from django.template.backends.django import DjangoTemplates
from django.test.utils import override_settings

# Check what the default template dirs are
print('Before override:')
for backend in engines.all():
    if isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
        print('  dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)

with override_settings(TEMPLATES=[{
    'DIRS': [''],
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    print('During override:')
    for backend in engines.all():
        if isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            print('  dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)
    
    from django.template import autoreload
    dirs = autoreload.get_template_directories()
    print('  template dirs:', dirs)
    
    # Let's manually check
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    items = set()
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue
        print('  processing dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)
        items.update(cwd / Path(dir) for dir in backend.engine.dirs if dir)
    print('  manual items:', items)
""}

import django
django.setup()

from django.template import engines
from django.template.backends.django import DjangoTemplates
from django.test.utils import override_settings
from pathlib import Path

# Check what the default template dirs are
print('Before override:')
for backend in engines.all():
    if isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
        print('  dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)

with override_settings(TEMPLATES=[{
    'DIRS': [''],
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    print('During override:')
    for backend in engines.all():
        if isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            print('  dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)
    
    from django.template import autoreload
    dirs = autoreload.get_template_directories()
    print('  template dirs:', dirs)
    
    # Let's manually check
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    items = set()
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue
        print('  processing dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)
        items.update(cwd / Path(dir) for dir in backend.engine.dirs if dir)
    print('  manual items:', items)
""}

import os

import django
django.setup()

from django.template import engines
from django.template.backends.django import DjangoTemplates
from django.test.utils import override_settings
from pathlib import Path

# Check what the default template dirs are
print('Before override:')
for backend in engines.all():
    if isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
        print('  dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)

def get_template_directories():
    # Iterate through each template backend and find
    # any template_loader that has a 'get_dirs' method.
    # Collect the directories, filtering out Django templates.
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    items = set()
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue

        items.update(cwd / to_path(dir) for dir in backend.engine.dirs if dir)

        for loader in backend.engine.template_loaders:
            if not hasattr(loader, "get_dirs"):
                continue
            items.update(
                cwd / to_path(directory)
                for directory in loader.get_dirs()
                if not is_django_path(directory)
            )
    return items

{"command": "cd /testbed && python -c "
import importlib
import sys

# Force reload
for mod in list(sys.modules.keys()):
    if 'django' in mod:
        del sys.modules[mod]

import os
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
django.setup()

from django.template import engines
from django.template.backends.django import DjangoTemplates
from django.test.utils import override_settings
from pathlib import Path

# Check what the default template dirs are
print('Before override:')
for backend in engines.all():
    if isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
        print('  dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)
        print('  id:', id(backend))

with override_settings(TEMPLATES=[{
    'DIRS': [''],
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    print('During override:')
    for backend in engines.all():
        if isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            print('  dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)
            print('  id:', id(backend))
    
    # Now call get_template_directories
    from django.template import autoreload
    dirs = autoreload.get_template_directories()
    print('  get_template_directories result:', dirs)
    
    # Let's also manually do what the function does
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    items = set()
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue
        print('  backend.engine.dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)
        for dir in backend.engine.dirs:
            print(f'    dir={dir!r}, bool(dir)={bool(dir)}')
        items.update(cwd / autoreload.to_path(dir) for dir in backend.engine.dirs if dir)
    print('  manual items:', items)
""}

import os

import django
django.setup()

from django.template import engines
from django.template.backends.django import DjangoTemplates
from django.test.utils import override_settings
from pathlib import Path

# Check what the default template dirs are
print('Before override:')
for backend in engines.all():
    if isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
        print('  dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)

with override_settings(TEMPLATES=[{
    'DIRS': [''],
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    print('During override:')
    for backend in engines.all():
        if isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            print('  dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)
    
    # Now let's trace through get_template_directories step by step
    from django.template import autoreload
    
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    items = set()
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue
        print('  backend.engine.dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)
        # This is the exact line from the function
        items.update(cwd / autoreload.to_path(dir) for dir in backend.engine.dirs if dir)
    print('  items:', items)
    
    # Now call the actual function
    print('  function result:', autoreload.get_template_directories())
""}

import os

import django
django.setup()

from django.template import autoreload
from django.test.utils import override_settings
from pathlib import Path

# Let's monkey-patch to see what's happening
original_get_template_directories = autoreload.get_template_directories

def debug_get_template_directories():
    from django.template import engines
    from django.template.backends.django import DjangoTemplates
    from django.utils._os import to_path
    
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    items = set()
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue
        print('DEBUG dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)
        items.update(cwd / to_path(dir) for dir in backend.engine.dirs if dir)
    print('DEBUG items:', items)
    return items

autoreload.get_template_directories = debug_get_template_directories

with override_settings(TEMPLATES=[{
    'DIRS': [''],
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    print('Result:', autoreload.get_template_directories())
""}

import functools
from collections import Counter
from pathlib import Path

from django.apps import apps
from django.conf import settings
from django.core.exceptions import ImproperlyConfigured
from django.utils.functional import cached_property
from django.utils.module_loading import import_string


class InvalidTemplateEngineError(ImproperlyConfigured):
    pass


class EngineHandler:
    def __init__(self, templates=None):
        """
        templates is an optional list of template engine definitions
        (structured like settings.TEMPLATES).
        """
        self._templates = templates
        self._engines = {}

    @cached_property
    def templates(self):
        if self._templates is None:
            self._templates = settings.TEMPLATES

        templates = {}
        backend_names = []
        for tpl in self._templates:
            try:
                # This will raise an exception if 'BACKEND' doesn't exist or
                # isn't a string containing at least one dot.
                default_name = tpl["BACKEND"].rsplit(".", 2)[-2]
            except Exception:
                invalid_backend = tpl.get("BACKEND", "<not defined>")
                raise ImproperlyConfigured(
                    "Invalid BACKEND for a template engine: {}. Check "
                    "your TEMPLATES setting.".format(invalid_backend)
                )

            tpl = {
                "NAME": default_name,
                "DIRS": [],
                "APP_DIRS": False,
                "OPTIONS": {},
                **tpl,
            }

            templates[tpl["NAME"]] = tpl
            backend_names.append(tpl["NAME"])

        counts = Counter(backend_names)
        duplicates = [alias for alias, count in counts.most_common() if count > 1]
        if duplicates:
            raise ImproperlyConfigured(
                "Template engine aliases aren't unique, duplicates: {}. "
                "Set a unique NAME for each engine in settings.TEMPLATES.".format(
                    ", ".join(duplicates)
                )
            )

        return templates

    def __getitem__(self, alias):
        try:
            return self._engines[alias]
        except KeyError:
            try:
                params = self.templates[alias]
            except KeyError:
                raise InvalidTemplateEngineError(
                    "Could not find config for '{}' "
                    "in settings.TEMPLATES".format(alias)
                )

            # If importing or initializing the backend raises an exception,
            # self._engines[alias] isn't set and this code may get executed
            # again, so we must preserve the original params. See #24265.
            params = params.copy()
            backend = params.pop("BACKEND")
            engine_cls = import_string(backend)
            engine = engine_cls(params)

            self._engines[alias] = engine
            return engine

    def __iter__(self):
        return iter(self.templates)

    def all(self):
        return [self[alias] for alias in self]


@functools.lru_cache
def get_app_template_dirs(dirname):
    """
    Return an iterable of paths of directories to load app templates from.

    dirname is the name of the subdirectory containing templates inside
    installed applications.
    """
    template_dirs = [
        Path(app_config.path) / dirname
        for app_config in apps.get_app_configs()
        if app_config.path and (Path(app_config.path) / dirname).is_dir()
    ]
    # Immutable return value because it will be cached and shared by callers.
    return tuple(template_dirs)

def get_template_directories():
    # Iterate through each template backend and find
    # any template_loader that has a 'get_dirs' method.
    # Collect the directories, filtering out Django templates.
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    items = set()
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue

        items.update(cwd / to_path(dir) for dir in backend.engine.dirs if dir)

        for loader in backend.engine.template_loaders:
            if not hasattr(loader, "get_dirs"):
                continue
            items.update(
                cwd / to_path(directory)
                for directory in loader.get_dirs()
                if not is_django_path(directory)
            )
    return items

{"command": "cd /testbed && python3 -c "
import os
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
django.setup()

from django.template import engines
from django.template.backends.django import DjangoTemplates
from django.test.utils import override_settings
from pathlib import Path

# First, let's see what happens without override
print('Without override:')
for backend in engines.all():
    if isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
        print('  dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)

# Now with override
with override_settings(TEMPLATES=[{
    'DIRS': [''],
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    print('During override:')
    for backend in engines.all():
        if isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            print('  dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)
    
    from django.template import autoreload
    
    # Let's manually check what the function does
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    items = set()
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue
        print('  processing dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)
        # The exact line from the function
        items.update(cwd / autoreload.to_path(dir) for dir in backend.engine.dirs if dir)
    print('  manual items:', items)
    
    # Now call the function
    print('  function result:', autoreload.get_template_directories())
    
    # Let's also check if the function uses a different 'engines'
    print('  function globals engines id:', id(autoreload.get_template_directories.__globals__['engines']))
    print('  module engines id:', id(autoreload.engines))
    print('  local engines id:', id(engines))
""}

import os
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
django.setup()

from django.template import autoreload
from django.test.utils import override_settings
from pathlib import Path

with override_settings(TEMPLATES=[{
    'DIRS': [''],
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    # Let's manually trace through the function
    from django.template import engines
    from django.template.backends.django import DjangoTemplates
    from django.utils._os import to_path
    
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    items = set()
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue
        print('dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)
        # Let's check what the generator produces
        gen = (cwd / to_path(dir) for dir in backend.engine.dirs if dir)
        print('gen:', list(gen))
        
        # Now let's check without filter
        gen2 = (cwd / to_path(dir) for dir in backend.engine.dirs)
        print('gen2:', list(gen2))
        
        # Now use update
        items.update(cwd / to_path(dir) for dir in backend.engine.dirs if dir)
    print('items:', items)
""}

Source from inspect:
def get_template_directories():
    # Iterate through each template backend and find
    # any template_loader that has a 'get_dirs' method.
    # Collect the directories, filtering out Django templates.
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    items = set()
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue

        items.update(cwd / to_path(dir) for dir in backend.engine.dirs if dir)

        for loader in backend.engine.template_loaders:
            if not hasattr(loader, "get_dirs"):
                continue
            items.update(
                cwd / to_path(directory)
                for directory in loader.get_dirs()
                if not is_django_path(directory)
            )

import os

import django
django.setup()

from django.template import engines
from django.template.backends.django import DjangoTemplates
from django.test.utils import override_settings
from pathlib import Path

# First, let's see what happens without override
print('Without override:')
print('  _engines:', engines._engines)
for backend in engines.all():
    if isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
        print('  dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)

# Now with override
with override_settings(TEMPLATES=[{
    'DIRS': [''],
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    print('During override:')
    print('  _engines:', engines._engines)
    for backend in engines.all():
        if isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            print('  dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)
    
    from django.template import autoreload
    
    # Let's check what the function does
    print()
    print('Calling get_template_directories...')
    result = autoreload.get_template_directories()
    print('Result:', result)
    
    # Now let's check if the engines are still the same
    print()
    print('After function call:')
    print('  _engines:', engines._engines)
    for backend in engines.all():
        if isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            print('  dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)
""}

import dis, marshal, struct, time

with open('django/template/__pycache__/autoreload.cpython-311.pyc', 'rb') as f:
    magic = f.read(4)
    flags = struct.unpack('<I', f.read(4))[0]
    if flags & 0x1:
        hash = f.read(8)
    else:
        timestamp = f.read(4)
        size = f.read(4)
    code = marshal.load(f)

# Find the get_template_directories function

The bytecode in the .pyc file has the `POP_JUMP_BACKWARD_IF_FALSE` at offset 16 which is the `if dir` check. So the bytecode is correct.

Let me try a completely different approach - let me check if maybe the issue is that `override_settings` doesn't properly affect the `engines` object because the engines are cached:

function.arguments:
{"command": "cd /testbed && python3 -c "
import os
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
django.setup()

from django.template import engines
from django.template.backends.django import DjangoTemplates
from django.test.utils import override_settings
from pathlib import Path

# Let's check what happens when we access settings.TEMPLATES directly
from django.conf import settings

print('Default TEMPLATES:')
for t in settings.TEMPLATES:
    print(' ', t)

with override_settings(TEMPLATES=[{
    'DIRS': [''],
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    print('During override TEMPLATES:')
    for t in settings.TEMPLATES:
        print(' ', t)
    
    # Now check engines
    print('engines._engines:', engines._engines)
    print('engines.templates:', engines.templates)
    
    # Now call all()
    for backend in engines.all():
        if isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            print('backend.engine.dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)
    
    from django.template import autoreload
    print('function result:', autoreload.get_template_directories())
""}

import os
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
django.setup()

from django.template import engines
from django.template.backends.django import DjangoTemplates
from django.test.utils import override_settings
from pathlib import Path

# Let's check if the issue is that the engine is created with the wrong dirs
# by looking at how DjangoTemplates handles the params

# First, let's see what happens when we create a DjangoTemplates backend directly
from django.template.backends.django import DjangoTemplates as DjangoTemplatesBackend

# Create one with empty string dirs
backend = DjangoTemplatesBackend({
    'DIRS': [''],
    'APP_DIRS': False,
    'NAME': 'test',
    'OPTIONS': {},
})
print('Backend dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)

# Now test the filter
cwd = Path.cwd()
items = set()
items.update(cwd / Path(d) for d in backend.engine.dirs if d)
print('Items with filter:', items)

items2 = set()
items2.update(cwd / Path(d) for d in backend.engine.dirs)
print('Items without filter:', items2)
""}

from django.template import autoreload

from pathlib import Path

src = inspect.getsource(autoreload.get_template_directories)

# Now there should be a new function in the globals
new_func = autoreload.get_template_directories.__globals__['get_template_directories']
print('New function:', new_func)

    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    print('Original function result:', autoreload.get_template_directories())
    print('New function result:', new_func())

import django
django.setup()

from django.template import autoreload
from django.test.utils import override_settings
from pathlib import Path

# Let's just write a completely independent function
def my_get_template_directories():
    from django.template import engines
    from django.template.backends.django import DjangoTemplates
    from django.utils._os import to_path
    from pathlib import Path
    
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    items = set()
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue
        items.update(cwd / to_path(dir) for dir in backend.engine.dirs if dir)
        
        for loader in backend.engine.template_loaders:
            if not hasattr(loader, 'get_dirs'):
                continue
            items.update(
                cwd / to_path(directory)
                for directory in loader.get_dirs()
                if not is_django_path(directory)
            )
    return items

with override_settings(TEMPLATES=[{
    'DIRS': [''],
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    print('Original function result:', autoreload.get_template_directories())
    print('My function result:', my_get_template_directories())

The bytecode looks correct. The generator has `POP_JUMP_BACKWARD_IF_FALSE` at offset 16 which checks `if dir`.

Wait, I just noticed something. Let me look at the main function bytecode more carefully. At offset 156-230:

```
156 LOAD_FAST                0 (items)
158 LOAD_METHOD              7 (update)
...
188 LOAD_FAST                1 (backend)
190 LOAD_ATTR                8 (engine)
200 LOAD_ATTR                9 (dirs)
210 GET_ITER
212 PRECALL                  0
216 CALL                     0
226 PRECALL                  1
230 CALL                     1
```

Wait, this is calling `update()` with the result of calling the generator. But the generator is created at offset 184-186 (`MAKE_FUNCTION`) and then called at 212-216. Let me trace through this more carefully...

Actually, I think I see the issue now. At offset 210, `GET_ITER` gets an iterator from `backend.engine.dirs`. Then at 212-216, it calls the generator function with the iterator as argument. But wait, the generator function is created at 184-186 with a closure containing `cwd`. Then at 188-210, it loads `backend.engine.dirs` and gets an iterator. Then at 212-216, it calls the generator with the iterator.

messages.content:
I see a key difference! In the inline code, the generator expression at line 7 has:

```
12 LOAD_FAST                1 (d)
14 POP_JUMP_BACKWARD_IF_FALSE     4 (to 8)
```

This checks `if d` and jumps back to the FOR_ITER if d is falsy.

But in the function's generator (from the module), the bytecode is:

```
14 LOAD_FAST                1 (dir)
16 POP_JUMP_BACKWARD_IF_FALSE     4 (to 10)
```

Same thing. So the bytecode is correct.

Wait, but there's another difference. In the inline code, the generator uses `LOAD_GLOBAL 0 (cwd)` to access `cwd`, while in the function's generator, it uses `LOAD_DEREF 2 (cwd)` because `cwd` is a closure variable.

Let me check if maybe the issue is with the closure. Let me check if `cwd` is being captured correctly:

{"command": "cd /testbed && python3 -c "
import os
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
django.setup()

from django.template import autoreload
from django.test.utils import override_settings
from pathlib import Path

# Let's monkey-patch the function to add debugging
original_func = autoreload.get_template_directories

def debug_func():
    from django.template import engines
    from django.template.backends.django import DjangoTemplates
    from django.utils._os import to_path
    from pathlib import Path
    
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    print('DEBUG cwd:', cwd)
    items = set()
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue
        print('DEBUG dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)
        for d in backend.engine.dirs:
            print(f'DEBUG d={d!r}, bool(d)={bool(d)}')
        items.update(cwd / to_path(d) for d in backend.engine.dirs if d)
    print('DEBUG items:', items)
    return items

autoreload.get_template_directories = debug_func

with override_settings(TEMPLATES=[{
    'DIRS': [''],
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    print('Result:', autoreload.get_template_directories())
""}

from django.template import autoreload
from django.test.utils import override_settings
from pathlib import Path

import importlib.util
spec = importlib.util.find_spec('django.template.autoreload')

import os
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
django.setup()

from django.template import engines
from django.template.backends.django import DjangoTemplates
from django.test.utils import override_settings
from pathlib import Path

# Let's check what happens when we call the function multiple times
from django.template import autoreload

# First call without override
print('Without override:', autoreload.get_template_directories())

# Now with override
with override_settings(TEMPLATES=[{
    'DIRS': [''],
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    print('During override:', autoreload.get_template_directories())
    
    # Let's also check what engines.all() returns
    print('engines.all():', engines.all())
    for b in engines.all():
        if isinstance(b, DjangoTemplates):
            print('  dirs:', b.engine.dirs)
    
    # Let's manually check
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    items = set()
    for b in engines.all():
        if isinstance(b, DjangoTemplates):
            items.update(cwd / Path(d) for d in b.engine.dirs if d)
    print('Manual:', items)

# After override
print('After override:', autoreload.get_template_directories())
""}

import django

from django.template import engines
from django.template.backends.django import DjangoTemplates
from django.test.utils import override_settings
from pathlib import Path

# Let's check what happens when we call the function multiple times
from django.template import autoreload

# First call without override
print('Without override:', autoreload.get_template_directories())

# Now with override
with override_settings(TEMPLATES=[{
    'DIRS': [''],

}]):

OK, let me try a completely different approach. Let me check if maybe the issue is that the `override_settings` context manager doesn't properly reset the `engines._engines` cache, and the function is using a cached engine from a previous call:

Actually, let me re-read the PR description more carefully:

> Django 3.2.4+ autoreload breaks on empty string in TEMPLATES DIRS.
> ...
> This happens because django/template/autoreload.py::template_changed was previously comparing the empty string to a directory, and would never match. Now the normalization transforms the empty string into the root of the project.

So the issue is that `to_path('')` returns `Path('.')` which when combined with `cwd / Path('.')` becomes `cwd`. This causes `template_changed` to always return True.

The fix should filter out empty strings from the template directories. Let me check if maybe the issue is that my fix is not being applied correctly because of some caching issue:

import os
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
django.setup()

from django.template import engines
from django.template.backends.django import DjangoTemplates
from django.test.utils import override_settings
from pathlib import Path

# Let's check if the issue is that the engine is created with the wrong dirs
# by looking at how DjangoTemplates handles the params

# First, let's see what happens when we access engines.all() without override
print('Before override:')
print('  _engines:', engines._engines)
print('  all():', engines.all())

with override_settings(TEMPLATES=[{
    'DIRS': [''],
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    print()
    print('During override:')
    print('  _engines:', engines._engines)
    
    # Let's check if the engine is created with the correct dirs
    # by looking at the backend's engine
    for backend in engines.all():
        if isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            print('  backend.engine.dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)
            print('  backend.engine.dirs[0]:', repr(backend.engine.dirs[0]))
            
            # Let's check the engine's internal state
            print('  backend.engine:', backend.engine)
            print('  dirs type:', type(backend.engine.dirs))
    
    from django.template import autoreload
    
    # Let's check if the function's code is correct
    import inspect
    src = inspect.getsource(autoreload.get_template_directories)
    print()
    print('Source:')
    print(src)
    
    # Let's also check if there's a difference between calling the function
    # and executing the same code inline
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    items = set()
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue
        items.update(cwd / autoreload.to_path(d) for d in backend.engine.dirs if d)
    print('Inline result:', items)
    
    print('Function result:', autoreload.get_template_directories())
""}

from pathlib import Path

    'DIRS': [''],
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    print('Original result:', autoreload.get_template_directories())
    print('New result:', new_func())

from django.template import engines
from django.template.backends.django import DjangoTemplates
from django.test.utils import override_settings

with override_settings(TEMPLATES=[{
    'DIRS': [''],

}]):

    cwd = Path.cwd()

        print('  dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)
        items.update(cwd / Path(d) for d in backend.engine.dirs if d)
    print('  result:', items)
    
    # Now let's define a function and call it
    def test_func():
        cwd = Path.cwd()
        items = set()
        for backend in engines.all():
            if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
                continue
            items.update(cwd / Path(d) for d in backend.engine.dirs if d)
        return items
    
    print('Function:')
    print('  result:', test_func())

import django

from django.template import autoreload
from django.test.utils import override_settings
from pathlib import Path

# by looking at the actual bytes

func = autoreload.get_template_directories

# Let's look at the generator expression code
for const in code.co_consts:
    if hasattr(const, 'co_name') and const.co_name == '<genexpr>':
        print('Generator code:')
        print('  co_code:', const.co_code.hex())
        print('  co_stacksize:', const.co_stacksize)
        print('  co_nlocals:', const.co_nlocals)
        print('  co_argcount:', const.co_argcount)
        print('  co_kwonlyargcount:', const.co_kwonlyargcount)
        print('  co_flags:', const.co_flags)
        
        # Let's check if the bytecode has the POP_JUMP_BACKWARD_IF_FALSE instruction
        # In Python 3.11, this is opcode 175
        code_bytes = const.co_code
        has_pop_jump = False
        for i in range(0, len(code_bytes), 2):
            if code_bytes[i] == 175:  # POP_JUMP_BACKWARD_IF_FALSE
                has_pop_jump = True
                print(f'  Found POP_JUMP_BACKWARD_IF_FALSE at offset {i}')
        if not has_pop_jump:
            print('  WARNING: No POP_JUMP_BACKWARD_IF_FALSE found!')

import os
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
django.setup()

from django.template import engines
from django.template.backends.django import DjangoTemplates
from django.test.utils import override_settings
from pathlib import Path

# Let's check if the issue is that the engines are cached from a previous call
# by checking the _engines cache before and after calling the function

print('Before any override:')
print('  _engines:', engines._engines)

with override_settings(TEMPLATES=[{
    'DIRS': [''],
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    print()
    print('During override (before function call):')
    print('  _engines:', engines._engines)
    
    from django.template import autoreload
    
    # Call the function
    result = autoreload.get_template_directories()
    print('  function result:', result)
    
    print()
    print('After function call:')
    print('  _engines:', engines._engines)
    
    # Now let's check if the engine has the correct dirs
    for alias, engine in engines._engines.items():
        if isinstance(engine, DjangoTemplates):
            print(f'  engine {alias} dirs:', engine.engine.dirs)
    
    # Now let's manually check
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    items = set()
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue
        items.update(cwd / Path(d) for d in backend.engine.dirs if d)
    print('  manual result:', items)
""}

from pathlib import Path

from django.dispatch import receiver
from django.template import engines
from django.template.backends.django import DjangoTemplates
from django.utils._os import to_path
from django.utils.autoreload import autoreload_started, file_changed, is_django_path


def get_template_directories():
    # Iterate through each template backend and find
    # any template_loader that has a 'get_dirs' method.
    # Collect the directories, filtering out Django templates.
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    items = set()
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue

        items.update(cwd / to_path(dir) for dir in backend.engine.dirs if dir)

        for loader in backend.engine.template_loaders:
            if not hasattr(loader, "get_dirs"):
                continue
            items.update(
                cwd / to_path(directory)
                for directory in loader.get_dirs()
                if not is_django_path(directory)
            )
    return items


def reset_loaders():
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue
        for loader in backend.engine.template_loaders:
            loader.reset()


@receiver(autoreload_started, dispatch_uid="template_loaders_watch_changes")
def watch_for_template_changes(sender, **kwargs):
    for directory in get_template_directories():
        sender.watch_dir(directory, "**/*")


@receiver(file_changed, dispatch_uid="template_loaders_file_changed")
def template_changed(sender, file_path, **kwargs):
    if file_path.suffix == ".py":
        return
    for template_dir in get_template_directories():
        if template_dir in file_path.parents:
            reset_loaders()
            return True

from pathlib import Path

from django.dispatch import receiver
from django.template import engines
from django.template.backends.django import DjangoTemplates
from django.utils._os import to_path
from django.utils.autoreload import autoreload_started, file_changed, is_django_path


def get_template_directories():
    # Iterate through each template backend and find
    # any template_loader that has a 'get_dirs' method.
    # Collect the directories, filtering out Django templates.
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    items = set()
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue

        items.update(cwd / to_path(dir) for dir in backend.engine.dirs if dir)

        for loader in backend.engine.template_loaders:
            if not hasattr(loader, "get_dirs"):
                continue
            items.update(
                cwd / to_path(directory)
                for directory in loader.get_dirs()
                if not is_django_path(directory)
            )
    return items


def reset_loaders():
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue
        for loader in backend.engine.template_loaders:
            loader.reset()


@receiver(autoreload_started, dispatch_uid="template_loaders_watch_changes")
def watch_for_template_changes(sender, **kwargs):
    for directory in get_template_directories():
        sender.watch_dir(directory, "**/*")


@receiver(file_changed, dispatch_uid="template_loaders_file_changed")
def template_changed(sender, file_path, **kwargs):
    if file_path.suffix == ".py":
        return
    for template_dir in get_template_directories():
        if template_dir in file_path.parents:
            reset_loaders()
            return True

The bytecode has the `POP_JUMP_BACKWARD_IF_FALSE` at offset 16 in the generator. This is the `if dir` check. The bytecode is correct.

But the function still returns the wrong result. Let me check if maybe there's a problem with the `engines.all()` method:

function.arguments:
{"command": "cd /testbed && python3 -c "
import os
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
django.setup()

from django.template import autoreload
from django.test.utils import override_settings
from pathlib import Path

# Let's check if the function is actually using the correct engines
# by checking the function's globals

func = autoreload.get_template_directories
print('Function globals engines:', func.__globals__['engines'])
print('Function globals engines id:', id(func.__globals__['engines']))

# Let's check the module's engines
print('Module engines:', autoreload.engines)
print('Module engines id:', id(autoreload.engines))

# Let's check if they're the same
print('Same?', func.__globals__['engines'] is autoreload.engines)

# Let's check if the function's engines.all() returns the same as the module's engines.all()
with override_settings(TEMPLATES=[{
    'DIRS': [''],
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    print()
    print('During override:')
    print('Function engines.all():', func.__globals__['engines'].all())
    print('Module engines.all():', autoreload.engines.all())
    
    # Check the dirs
    for b in func.__globals__['engines'].all():
        if isinstance(b, autoreload.DjangoTemplates):
            print('Function backend dirs:', b.engine.dirs)
    
    for b in autoreload.engines.all():
        if isinstance(b, autoreload.DjangoTemplates):
            print('Module backend dirs:', b.engine.dirs)
    
    # Now call the function
    print()
    print('Function result:', autoreload.get_template_directories())
""}

def get_template_directories():
    # Iterate through each template backend and find
    # any template_loader that has a 'get_dirs' method.
    # Collect the directories, filtering out Django templates.
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    items = set()
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue

        items.update(cwd / to_path(dir) for dir in backend.engine.dirs if dir)

        for loader in backend.engine.template_loaders:
            if not hasattr(loader, "get_dirs"):
                continue
            items.update(
                cwd / to_path(directory)
                for directory in loader.get_dirs()
                if not is_django_path(directory)
            )
    return items

def get_template_directories():
    # Iterate through each template backend and find
    # any template_loader that has a 'get_dirs' method.
    # Collect the directories, filtering out Django templates.
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    items = set()
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue

        items.update(cwd / to_path(dir) for dir in backend.engine.dirs if dir)

        for loader in backend.engine.template_loaders:
            if not hasattr(loader, "get_dirs"):
                continue
            items.update(
                cwd / to_path(directory)
                for directory in loader.get_dirs()
                if not is_django_path(directory)
            )
    return items

<output>
from pathlib import Path

from django.dispatch import receiver
from django.template import engines
from django.template.backends.django import DjangoTemplates
from django.utils._os import to_path
from django.utils.autoreload import autoreload_started, file_changed, is_django_path


def get_template_directories():
    # Iterate through each template backend and find
    # any template_loader that has a 'get_dirs' method.
    # Collect the directories, filtering out Django templates.
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    items = set()
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue

        for dir in backend.engine.dirs:
            if dir:
                items.add(cwd / to_path(dir))

        for loader in backend.engine.template_loaders:
            if not hasattr(loader, "get_dirs"):
                continue
            items.update(
                cwd / to_path(directory)
                for directory in loader.get_dirs()
                if not is_django_path(directory)
            )
    return items


def reset_loaders():
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue
        for loader in backend.engine.template_loaders:
            loader.reset()


@receiver(autoreload_started, dispatch_uid="template_loaders_watch_changes")
def watch_for_template_changes(sender, **kwargs):
    for directory in get_template_directories():
        sender.watch_dir(directory, "**/*")


@receiver(file_changed, dispatch_uid="template_loaders_file_changed")
def template_changed(sender, file_path, **kwargs):
    if file_path.suffix == ".py":
        return
    for template_dir in get_template_directories():
        if template_dir in file_path.parents:
            reset_loaders()
            return True

import os

import django
django.setup()

from django.template import autoreload
from django.test.utils import override_settings
from pathlib import Path

# Test 1: Empty string in DIRS should not include cwd
print('Test 1: Empty string in DIRS')
with override_settings(TEMPLATES=[{
    'DIRS': [''],
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    dirs = autoreload.get_template_directories()
    print('  Template directories:', dirs)
    print('  cwd in dirs:', Path.cwd() in dirs)
    if Path.cwd() in dirs:
        print('  FAIL: cwd should not be in dirs')
    else:
        print('  PASS')

# Test 2: Normal directories still work

    'DIRS': ['/tmp/test_templates'],
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    dirs = autoreload.get_template_directories()
    print('  Template directories:', dirs)
    if Path('/tmp/test_templates') in dirs:
        print('  PASS')

def get_template_directories():
    # Iterate through each template backend and find
    # any template_loader that has a 'get_dirs' method.
    # Collect the directories, filtering out Django templates.
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    items = set()
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue

        for dir in backend.engine.dirs:
            if dir:
                items.add(cwd / to_path(dir))

        for loader in backend.engine.template_loaders:
            if not hasattr(loader, "get_dirs"):
                continue
            items.update(
                cwd / to_path(directory)
                for directory in loader.get_dirs()
                if not is_django_path(directory)
            )
    return items

Wait, I just noticed something in the bytecode. At offset 186, there's `POP_JUMP_FORWARD_IF_FALSE 37 (to 262)`. This means if `dir` is falsy, it jumps to offset 262, which is the `JUMP_BACKWARD` to continue the loop. This is correct!

But wait, let me check the bytecode more carefully. At offset 180, there's `FOR_ITER 41 (to 264)`. This means the FOR_ITER at offset 180 jumps to 264 when the iterator is exhausted. But the `POP_JUMP_FORWARD_IF_FALSE` at offset 186 jumps to 262, which is `JUMP_BACKWARD 42 (to 180)`. So if `dir` is falsy, it jumps back to the FOR_ITER to get the next item. This is correct!

from django.template import autoreload
from django.test.utils import override_settings
from pathlib import Path

func = autoreload.get_template_directories

with override_settings(TEMPLATES=[{
    'DIRS': [''],
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    # Call the function's engines.all()
    func_engines = func.__globals__['engines']
    func_all = func_engines.all()
    print('Function engines.all():', func_all)
    
    # Call the module's engines.all()
    mod_all = autoreload.engines.all()
    print('Module engines.all():', mod_all)
    
    # Check if they're the same
    print('Same?', func_all == mod_all)
    print('Same objects?', [id(e) for e in func_all] == [id(e) for e in mod_all])
    
    # Check the dirs
    for backend in func_all:
        if isinstance(backend, autoreload.DjangoTemplates):
            print('Function backend dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)
    
    for backend in mod_all:
        if isinstance(backend, autoreload.DjangoTemplates):
            print('Module backend dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)
    
    # Now call the function
    print('Function result:', autoreload.get_template_directories())

But wait, `cached_property` is not re-evaluated automatically when the underlying setting changes. It's only re-evaluated if the cache is cleared. Let me check if `override_settings` clears the cache:

function.arguments:
{"command": "cd /testbed && python3 -c "
import sys
for mod in list(sys.modules.keys()):
    if 'django' in mod:
        del sys.modules[mod]

import os
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
django.setup()

from django.conf import settings
from django.template import engines
from django.template.backends.django import DjangoTemplates
from django.test.utils import override_settings
from pathlib import Path

# Let's check if the templates property is cached
print('Before override:')
print('  engines.templates:', engines.templates)
print('  engines.__dict__:', list(engines.__dict__.keys()))

with override_settings(TEMPLATES=[{
    'DIRS': [''],
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    print()
    print('During override:')
    print('  engines.templates:', engines.templates)
    print('  engines.__dict__:', list(engines.__dict__.keys()))
    
    # Check if 'templates' is in __dict__ (which means it's cached)
    print('  templates in __dict__:', 'templates' in engines.__dict__)
""}

messages.content:
Interesting! The `override_settings` context manager clears the `templates` cached property (it's no longer in `__dict__` during the override). So the `templates` property is re-evaluated with the overridden settings.

But wait, the `_engines` cache is NOT cleared. Let me check:

function.arguments:
{"command": "cd /testbed && python3 -c "
import sys
for mod in list(sys.modules.keys()):
    if 'django' in mod:
        del sys.modules[mod]

import os
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
django.setup()

from django.conf import settings
from django.template import engines
from django.template.backends.django import DjangoTemplates
from django.test.utils import override_settings
from pathlib import Path

# Let's check if _engines is cleared during override
print('Before override:')
print('  _engines:', engines._engines)

# Populate the cache
engines.all()
print('  _engines after all():', engines._engines)

with override_settings(TEMPLATES=[{
    'DIRS': [''],
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    print()
    print('During override:')
    print('  _engines:', engines._engines)
    print('  _templates:', engines._templates)
    
    # The _engines cache is NOT cleared! It still has the old engine.
    # But the old engine was created with the default TEMPLATES (which is [])
    # So the old engine should have dirs=[] not dirs=['']
    
    from django.template import autoreload
    print('  Function result:', autoreload.get_template_directories())
""}

function.arguments:
{"command": "cd /testbed && python -c "
import os
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
django.setup()

from django.template import autoreload
from django.test.utils import override_settings
from pathlib import Path

# Test 1: Empty string in DIRS should not include cwd
print('Test 1: Empty string in DIRS')
with override_settings(TEMPLATES=[{
    'DIRS': [''],
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    dirs = autoreload.get_template_directories()
    print('  Template directories:', dirs)
    print('  cwd in dirs:', Path.cwd() in dirs)
    if Path.cwd() in dirs:
        print('  FAIL: cwd should not be in dirs')
    else:
        print('  PASS')

# Test 2: Normal directories still work
print('Test 2: Normal directories')
with override_settings(TEMPLATES=[{
    'DIRS': ['/tmp/test_templates'],
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    dirs = autoreload.get_template_directories()
    print('  Template directories:', dirs)
    if Path('/tmp/test_templates') in dirs:
        print('  PASS')
    else:
        print('  FAIL: /tmp/test_templates should be in dirs')

# Test 3: Multiple dirs with some empty
print('Test 3: Mixed empty and valid dirs')
with override_settings(TEMPLATES=[{
    'DIRS': ['', '/tmp/valid_dir', ''],
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    dirs = autoreload.get_template_directories()
    print('  Template directories:', dirs)
    if Path.cwd() not in dirs and Path('/tmp/valid_dir') in dirs:
        print('  PASS')
    else:
        print('  FAIL')

messages.content:
The fix looks correct. It replaces the generator expression with a for loop that checks if `dir` is truthy (non-empty) before adding it to the items set.

Let me also check if the `template_changed` function is affected by this fix. The `template_changed` function calls `get_template_directories()` and checks if the file path is in the parents of any template directory. If an empty string in DIRS causes `get_template_directories()` to return the project root, then `template_changed` would always return True for any file change, which would break autoreload.

With my fix, empty strings are filtered out, so `get_template_directories()` won't return the project root, and `template_changed` will work correctly.

Let me also check if there are any other places in the codebase that might have the same issue:

{"command": "cd /testbed && python -c "
import os
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
django.setup()

from django.template import autoreload
from django.test.utils import override_settings
from pathlib import Path

# Test the fix
print('Testing fix for empty string in TEMPLATES DIRS...')

# Test 1: Empty string should be filtered out
with override_settings(TEMPLATES=[{
    'DIRS': [''],
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    dirs = autoreload.get_template_directories()
    assert Path.cwd() not in dirs, f'FAIL: cwd should not be in dirs, but got {dirs}'
    print('  PASS: Empty string filtered out')

# Test 2: Multiple empty strings should all be filtered out
with override_settings(TEMPLATES=[{
    'DIRS': ['', '', ''],
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    dirs = autoreload.get_template_directories()
    assert len(dirs) == 0, f'FAIL: dirs should be empty, but got {dirs}'
    print('  PASS: Multiple empty strings filtered out')

# Test 3: Mixed empty and valid dirs
with override_settings(TEMPLATES=[{
    'DIRS': ['', '/tmp/valid', ''],
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    dirs = autoreload.get_template_directories()
    assert Path('/tmp/valid') in dirs, f'FAIL: /tmp/valid should be in dirs, but got {dirs}'
    assert Path.cwd() not in dirs, f'FAIL: cwd should not be in dirs, but got {dirs}'
    print('  PASS: Mixed empty and valid dirs')

# Test 4: Normal dirs still work
with override_settings(TEMPLATES=[{
    'DIRS': ['/tmp/valid1', '/tmp/valid2'],
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    dirs = autoreload.get_template_directories()
    assert Path('/tmp/valid1') in dirs, f'FAIL: /tmp/valid1 should be in dirs'
    assert Path('/tmp/valid2') in dirs, f'FAIL: /tmp/valid2 should be in dirs'
    print('  PASS: Normal dirs work')

print()
print('All tests passed!')
""}

class TemplateReloadTests(SimpleTestCase):
    @mock.patch("django.template.autoreload.reset_loaders")
    def test_template_changed(self, mock_reset):
        template_path = Path(__file__).parent / "templates" / "index.html"
        self.assertTrue(autoreload.template_changed(None, template_path))
        mock_reset.assert_called_once()

    @mock.patch("django.template.autoreload.reset_loaders")
    def test_non_template_changed(self, mock_reset):
        self.assertIsNone(autoreload.template_changed(None, Path(__file__)))
        mock_reset.assert_not_called()

    @override_settings(
        TEMPLATES=[
            {

def get_template_directories():
    # Iterate through each template backend and find
    # any template_loader that has a 'get_dirs' method.
    # Collect the directories, filtering out Django templates.
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    items = set()
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue

        for dir in backend.engine.dirs:
            if dir:
                items.add(cwd / to_path(dir))

        for loader in backend.engine.template_loaders:
            if not hasattr(loader, "get_dirs"):
                continue
            items.update(
                cwd / to_path(directory)
                for directory in loader.get_dirs()
                if not is_django_path(directory)
            )
    return items

{"command": "cd /testbed && python -c "
import importlib
import sys

# Start fresh
for mod in list(sys.modules.keys()):
    if 'django' in mod:
        del sys.modules[mod]

import os
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
django.setup()

from django.template import autoreload
from django.test.utils import override_settings
from pathlib import Path

# Test just one thing
print('Test: Empty string in DIRS')
with override_settings(TEMPLATES=[{
    'DIRS': [''],
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    # Let's manually trace through the function
    from django.template import engines
    from django.template.backends.django import DjangoTemplates
    from django.utils._os import to_path
    
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    items = set()
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue
        print('  dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)
        for d in backend.engine.dirs:
            print(f'    d={d!r}, bool(d)={bool(d)}')
            if d:
                print(f'    adding {cwd / to_path(d)}')
                items.add(cwd / to_path(d))
    print('  manual result:', items)
    
    # Now call the function
    print('  function result:', autoreload.get_template_directories())
""}

def get_template_directories():
    # Iterate through each template backend and find
    # any template_loader that has a 'get_dirs' method.
    # Collect the directories, filtering out Django templates.
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    items = set()
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue

        for dir in backend.engine.dirs:
            if dir:
                items.add(cwd / to_path(dir))

        for loader in backend.engine.template_loaders:
            if not hasattr(loader, "get_dirs"):
                continue
            items.update(
                cwd / to_path(directory)
                for directory in loader.get_dirs()
                if not is_django_path(directory)
            )
    return items

from pathlib import Path

    from django.template import autoreload
    
    # Let's manually call the function step by step

    items = set()

messages.content:
The closure is `None` even after calling the function. This is because `MAKE_CELL` creates the cell at runtime, but the closure is only exposed if the function has free variables. Since the function has `co_cellvars = ('cwd',)` but no `co_freevars`, the cells are created but not exposed as a closure.

Wait, but the function has `co_cellvars = ('cwd',)` which means `cwd` is a cell variable. And the generator expression has `co_freevars = ('cwd',)` which means `cwd` is a free variable in the generator. So the generator should capture the cell from the enclosing function.

But the function's closure is `None`, which means the cells are not being created. This could be the issue!

But the function's closure is `None`, which means the cells are not being created. This could be the issue!

import importlib
import sys

for mod in list(sys.modules.keys()):
    if 'django' in mod:
        del sys.modules[mod]

import os
os.environ['DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE'] = 'tests.test_sqlite'

import django
django.setup()

from django.template import engines
from django.template.backends.django import DjangoTemplates
from django.test.utils import override_settings
from pathlib import Path

with override_settings(TEMPLATES=[{
    'DIRS': [''],
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    # Inline code
    print('Inline:')
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    items = set()
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue
        print('  dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)
        for d in backend.engine.dirs:
            print(f'    d={d!r}, bool(d)={bool(d)}')
            if d:
                items.add(cwd / Path(d))
    print('  result:', items)
    
    # Now define a function and call it
    def test_func():
        cwd = Path.cwd()
        items = set()
        for backend in engines.all():
            if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
                continue
            for d in backend.engine.dirs:
                if d:
                    items.add(cwd / Path(d))
        return items
    
    print('Function:')
    print('  result:', test_func())
"

import django
django.setup()

from django.template import autoreload
from django.test.utils import override_settings
from pathlib import Path

# Let's check if the function's engines is the same as the module's engines
func = autoreload.get_template_directories
print('Function engines:', func.__globals__['engines'])
print('Module engines:', autoreload.engines)
print('Same?', func.__globals__['engines'] is autoreload.engines)

# Let's check if the function's engines.all() returns the same as the module's engines.all()
with override_settings(TEMPLATES=[{
    'DIRS': [''],
    'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
}]):
    func_engines = func.__globals__['engines']
    mod_engines = autoreload.engines
    
    print()
    print('Function engines.all():', func_engines.all())
    print('Module engines.all():', mod_engines.all())
    
    # Check the dirs
    for backend in func_engines.all():
        if isinstance(backend, func.__globals__['DjangoTemplates']):
            print('Function backend dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)
    
    for backend in mod_engines.all():
        if isinstance(backend, autoreload.DjangoTemplates):
            print('Module backend dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)
    
    # Now let's manually call the function step by step
    print()
    print('Manual trace:')
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    items = set()
    for backend in func_engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, func.__globals__['DjangoTemplates']):
            continue
        print('  dirs:', backend.engine.dirs)
        for d in backend.engine.dirs:
            print(f'    d={d!r}, bool(d)={bool(d)}')
            if d:
                print(f'    adding {cwd / func.__globals__[\"to_path\"](d)}')
                items.add(cwd / func.__globals__['to_path'](d))
    print('  manual result:', items)
    
    print()
    print('Function result:', autoreload.get_template_directories())
""}

        for d in backend.engine.dirs:

                items.add(cwd / Path(d))
    print('Manual result:', items)

Source:
def get_template_directories():
    # Iterate through each template backend and find
    # any template_loader that has a 'get_dirs' method.
    # Collect the directories, filtering out Django templates.
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    items = set()
    for backend in engines.all():
        if not isinstance(backend, DjangoTemplates):
            continue

        for dir in backend.engine.dirs:
            if dir:
                items.add(cwd / to_path(dir))

        for loader in backend.engine.template_loaders:
            if not hasattr(loader, "get_dirs"):
                continue
            items.update(
                cwd / to_path(directory)
                for directory in loader.get_dirs()
                if not is_django_path(directory)
            )
    return items
